businessSummary.oneLiner: The world's largest EV maker and a 'Physical AI Platform' spanning autonomous driving, robotics, energy, and AI chips
businessSummary.description: Tesla extends beyond EV sales into FSD autonomous driving, Cybercab robotaxis, Optimus humanoid robots, and Megapack energy storage. FY2025 revenue hit $94.8B with GAAP EPS of $1.08. Q4 2025 gross margin reached 20.1%, a two-year high. Key growth catalysts including Cybercab production and Optimus Gen 3 kick off from April 2026.
howTheyMakeMoney: Services & charging (Supercharger network, insurance, OTA upgrades)

keyMetrics.name: P/E (TTM)
keyMetrics.interpretation: Extreme premium vs S&P 500 avg (23x) and auto sector avg (16x). Based on EPS $1.67. Reflects AI/robotics/energy future but valuation debate persists

keyMetrics.name: Forward P/E
keyMetrics.interpretation: Based on FY2026 EPS consensus $2.53. Barclays expects Q1 deliveries of 350K, warning of EV business deceleration

keyMetrics.name: Gross Margin
keyMetrics.interpretation: Q4 2025 hit two-year high of 20.1%. Margin recovery signal amid pricing pressure

keyMetrics.name: Revenue TTM
keyMetrics.interpretation: FY2025 annual revenue. Slight YoY decline but Energy segment driving growth

keyMetrics.name: FCF TTM
keyMetrics.interpretation: Healthy cash generation. $44B total cash to fund massive capex plans

keyMetrics.name: Net Margin
keyMetrics.interpretation: Compressed margins from price competition and R&D expansion

growthDrivers.title: Samsung AI6 Chip $16.5B Foundry Deal Confirmed
growthDrivers.description: Samsung confirmed at its AGM that it will produce Tesla's AI6 (HW6) chip at its Texas Taylor fab using 2nm (SF2P) process. $16.5B deal through 2033. However, AI6 MPW production is delayed ~6 months due to Samsung yield issues.

growthDrivers.title: Terafab Launch D-2: World's Largest 2nm AI Chip Fab
growthDrivers.description: Terafab is a vertically integrated fab handling logic, memory, and advanced packaging. $20B+ investment. March 21 launch marks groundbreaking/initial equipment installation.

growthDrivers.title: LG Energy Solution $4.3B LFP Battery Deal Confirmed
growthDrivers.description: US government confirmed the Michigan Lansing LFP cell manufacturing facility contract. US-made cells for Megapack 3, production starting 2027.

growthDrivers.title: Cybercab Production Starting April, Macrohard AI Venture
growthDrivers.description: Cybercab production line designed for hundreds per week. Macrohard (Tesla-xAI JV) enters enterprise AI as a new revenue stream beyond vehicles.

growthDrivers.title: China Jan-Feb Sales +35% YoY, Global Rebound Signal
growthDrivers.description: Tesla EV sales from China reached 127,728 units in Jan-Feb, up 35%+ YoY. BYD numbers actually declined, signaling a China market reversal.

competitiveAdvantage.summary: 50%+ US EV market share leader. 8.2B FSD miles, Supercharger NACS standard, and vertical integration form core moats. Samsung AI6 foundry deal accelerates custom chip ecosystem.

moats.description: 8.2 billion FSD miles. Overwhelmingly more training data than any competitor.
In-house batteries (4680), custom chips (Terafab + Samsung AI6), software (FSD), charging infrastructure (Supercharger).
NACS is the North American EV charging standard. GM, Ford, Rivian depend on Tesla Superchargers.

competitors.name: BYD
competitors.detail: Global EV sales leader (2.26M in 2025 vs Tesla 1.64M). 11 new models, 640-mile sedan, 5-min charging tech

recentNews.headline: Musk Twitter Trial Goes to Jury: Up to $2B in Damages at Stake
recentNews.significance: Shareholders allege Musk deliberately drove down Twitter stock during the 2022 acquisition. Jury deliberation could result in $2B+ damages

recentNews.headline: Musk and SEC in Active Settlement Talks Over Twitter Disclosures
recentNews.significance: Separate from shareholder trial. Both sides told the court 'further proceedings might not be necessary'

recentNews.headline: Barclays: Tesla EV Business Losing Investor Focus
recentNews.significance: Investor attention shifting to AI and future growth. Q1 deliveries estimated at 350K. $20B capex planned but core auto business matters

recentNews.headline: Samsung Confirms Tesla AI6 Chip 2nm Production at Texas Taylor Fab
recentNews.significance: Samsung AGM confirmed AI6 (HW6) foundry deal. 2027 H2 production target

recentNews.headline: US Government Confirms Tesla-LG $4.3B LFP Battery Deal
recentNews.significance: Megapack 3 battery cell factory. Major supply security for energy business

analystOpinions.notableComment: MarketBeat consensus $406.84. Hold rating across 43 analysts. Barclays warns of EV business losing focus while monitoring Q1 deliveries.

risks.title: Musk Twitter Trial $2B+ Risk
risks.description: Jury deliberation underway on whether Musk committed securities fraud during the 2022 Twitter acquisition. Damages could exceed $2B. SEC settlement talks also in progress separately.

risks.title: BYD Competition Intensifying
risks.description: BYD sold 2.26M EVs in 2025 (+28%) vs Tesla's 1.64M (-8%). 11 new models, 640-mile sedan, 5-min charging. Aggressive push into emerging markets.

risks.title: Barclays: EV Business Losing Focus, Q1 Uncertainty
risks.description: Barclays warns investor attention is shifting to AI while core EV business is overlooked. Q1 deliveries estimated at 350K. US sales weakness persists.

risks.title: Terafab + Samsung AI6 Execution Risk
risks.description: Tesla has zero semiconductor experience yet plans the world's largest 2nm fab. Samsung AI6 chip already delayed 6 months. $20B+ capex sunk cost risk.

risks.title: Stretched Valuation at P/E 235x
risks.description: TTM P/E 235x (EPS $1.67), 10x S&P 500 average. Net margin compressed to 4%. Multiple compression risk if growth disappoints.

risks.title: Capital Raise Risk
risks.description: $20B+ capex guidance for 2026. Electrek flags potential first secondary offering since 2020. Dilution risk.

buyReasons.title: Samsung AI6 $16.5B + LG $4.3B: Chip and Battery Supply Secured
buyReasons.rationale: Both AI chip foundry and Megapack battery cell supply confirmed simultaneously. Key bottlenecks for chip production and energy expansion are being resolved.

buyReasons.title: Terafab Launch D-2 + Cybercab April Production
buyReasons.rationale: Terafab groundbreaking in two days, Cybercab production next month. Near-term catalysts lined up.

buyReasons.title: China Sales +35%, Energy Business at Record Profit
buyReasons.rationale: China rebound signals global delivery recovery. Energy gross profit hit $1.1B, reducing EV dependency.

buyReasons.title: SEC Settlement Could Remove Overhang
buyReasons.rationale: Active settlement talks with SEC on Twitter disclosure case. 'Further proceedings may not be necessary' is a positive signal.

overallOpinion: The Musk Twitter trial has gone to the jury. Shareholders allege he deliberately tanked Twitter stock during the 2022 acquisition, with damages potentially exceeding $2B. SEC settlement talks are progressing separately, creating dual legal overhangs.

Barclays warned that investors are fixated on AI/robotics while ignoring the core EV business. Q1 deliveries are estimated at just 350K with US sales weakness persisting. Terafab launches in two days but meaningful revenue is still 1-2 years away.

The stock closed below its 200-day moving average ($393.85) at $392.78. The gap with the 50-day ($418.79) is widening. At P/E 235x with 43 analysts rating Hold, waiting for legal risk resolution is prudent.

investmentVerdict.summary: The Twitter trial $2B risk and SEC settlement create significant near-term uncertainty. Terafab groundbreaking (D-2) and Samsung AI6 confirmation are positive, but the break below the 200-day MA and Barclays' EV warning suggest waiting for legal resolution before entry.

sources.name: Meyka (Twitter Trial)
sources.description: Musk Twitter trial jury deliberation, $2B+ risk. P/E 235, EPS $1.67

sources.name: Yahoo Finance (Barclays)
sources.description: Barclays: EV business losing investor focus. Q1 deliveries 350K estimate

sources.name: SamMobile (Samsung AI6)
sources.description: Samsung AI6 chip Texas Taylor fab 2nm production confirmed

sources.name: Electrek (LG Battery)
sources.description: Tesla-LG $4.3B LFP battery factory deal confirmed

sources.name: ts2.tech
sources.description: Tesla near $400 after Samsung chip news

sources.name: Invezz
sources.description: Hotter inflation data pressuring Tesla stock

x.hook: Musk Twitter trial goes to jury, $2B damages at stake
x.text:
$TSLA (Mar 19) $392.78 (-1.63%). Musk Twitter trial is now with the jury. Up to $2B in damages. SEC settlement talks also underway. Stock fell below its 200-day MA.

1/4
thread:
Barclays warns investors are ignoring Tesla's core EV business. Q1 deliveries estimated at 350K. US sales weak. The bread-and-butter business needs attention.

2/4

Samsung AI6 $16.5B confirmed, LG battery $4.3B, Terafab D-2. Supply chain cards are strong but revenue realization is 1-2 years away.

3/4

P/E 235x, 43 analysts at Hold. Below 200-day MA ($393.85). No reason to rush until trial outcome. Target $407 (+4%).

4/4

threads.hook: Tesla faces $2B Twitter trial as stock breaks below 200-day MA
threads.text:
$TSLA (Mar 19) $392.78 (-1.63%)

The Musk Twitter trial has gone to jury deliberation. Shareholders allege he deliberately tanked the stock during the 2022 acquisition, with damages potentially exceeding $2B. SEC settlement talks are also in progress.

Barclays warns investors are ignoring the core EV business as they fixate on AI. Q1 deliveries estimated at 350K with US sales weakness.

Stock broke below the 200-day MA at $392.78. P/E 235x, 43 analysts Hold. Waiting for trial outcome and Q1 earnings (Apr 21) is prudent.

witty.hook: Musk is in court, in SEC talks, and launching Terafab. All at once.
witty.text:
$TSLA (Mar 19) $392.78 (-1.63%). Twitter trial went to jury: did Musk tank the stock on purpose? That's a $2B question. Meanwhile SEC wants to settle separately. Legal bills alone could fund a startup.

1/4
thread:
Barclays: investors only see AI robots. But most of the revenue comes from selling cars. Q1 needs 350K deliveries, and robots can't drive them to dealers yet.

2/4

Terafab groundbreaking in 2 days but the courtroom drama is getting more coverage. Samsung $16.5B, LG $4.3B. Cards are great, vibes are not.

3/4

Below the 200-day MA. P/E 235x, Hold consensus. Grab some popcorn and wait for the verdict.

4/4

empathy.hook: Tesla holders, a trial plus 200-day break. Tough day.
empathy.text:
$TSLA (Mar 19) $392.78 (-1.63%). Twitter trial at jury, $2B risk materializing. Stock broke below the 200-day MA. It's okay to feel uneasy.

1/4
thread:
SEC settlement talks progressing is a silver lining. 'Further proceedings may not be necessary.' One less overhang if resolved.

2/4

Terafab D-2, Samsung AI6, LG battery. Long-term cards remain strong. Short-term legal risk is just obscuring them.

3/4

RSI 36, near oversold. A not-guilty verdict could spark a sharp rebound. This is the patience zone.

4/4

witty.hook: Musk is juggling courtrooms and chip fabs simultaneously
witty.text:
$TSLA (Mar 19) $392.78 (-1.63%)

Twitter trial went to jury. $2B question: did Musk deliberately tank the stock? SEC also wants a separate settlement. The legal team must be billing overtime.

Barclays says investors only see AI. But most revenue comes from selling Model Ys. Q1 needs 350K deliveries, and no robot is doing that yet.

Below the 200-day MA, P/E 235x. Terafab launches in 2 days but the real drama is in the courtroom. Verdict first, then we talk.

empathy.hook: Tesla shareholders, trial plus 200-day break is rough
empathy.text:
$TSLA (Mar 19) $392.78 (-1.63%)

Twitter trial $2B risk plus breaking below the 200-day MA. If you're holding, this week feels especially long.

SEC settlement talks progressing is at least some comfort. One resolution at a time. Terafab, Samsung AI6, LG battery, all the long-term pieces are still strong.

Legal uncertainty is overshadowing everything right now. RSI 36 suggests selling pressure is overdone. If you can wait for the outcome, it may be worth it.
